strengths: useful for expectation mapping and timing

routing.guidance: Selects a tape-first proof style oriented around expectation mapping.

boundary.guidance: Slightly narrows attention toward what should show up in market behavior.

structure.guidance: Encourages structure that stays close to tradable expectation shifts and timing.

scenarios.guidance: Prefers scenarios that can be expressed through revisions, spreads, volatility, and price response.

question_generation.guidance: Favors questions like what will show up in revisions, spreads, or relative price action.

evidence_planning.guidance: Sets freshness bias, price-action priority, and conflict rules for noisy signals.

decision_logic.guidance: Can justify action on tape confirmation even when direct evidence is incomplete.

signal_translation.guidance: Favors price, vol, flows, revisions, skew, and relative-move signals.

monitoring.guidance: Monitoring should track confirmation, rejection, persistence, and crowding rather than one-off prints.

render.guidance: Usually only changes how explicitly expectation mapping is shown.
